title: OSS-ready: contributing docs, security, code of conduct, issue/PR templates
description:
LICENSE (MIT) is already in the repo. The rest of the open-source-readiness scaffolding is not. Add the missing files so the project is recognisable to GitHub's community standards check (https://github.com/<owner>/<repo>/community) and to first-time contributors arriving cold:

- CONTRIBUTING.md — how to set up, the TDD requirement (CLAUDE.md §3), the make ci gate, conventional commits (§11.6), branch naming, signing off.
- CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md — Contributor Covenant v2.1 boilerplate.
- SECURITY.md — how to report vulnerabilities privately; preferred contact channel (e-mail or GitHub private vulnerability reporting).
- CHANGELOG.md — keep-a-changelog format. Backfill v1.0.0 / v1.x rows from the existing release notes.
- .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/{bug_report,feature_request}.md — structured fields for repro / expected / actual / version / OS, plus a 'spec-mismatch' template that links to docs/spec-questions.md.
- .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md — checklist for tests / coverage / story-id reference / CHANGELOG entry / docs.
- README badge audit — confirm CI, Go reference, license badges all point at the right org/repo when the project moves to its public home.
- README 'Contributing' section — short paragraph linking to CONTRIBUTING.md.
- (Optional but cheap) .github/FUNDING.yml — empty or GitHub Sponsors placeholder so the sponsor button works.
acceptance_criteria: GitHub community-standards page reports 'All checks complete' once pushed
CONTRIBUTING.md documents the make ci gate and TDD requirement and links the spec
SECURITY.md states the disclosure channel and response SLA
Issue + PR templates exist; CHANGELOG.md backfilled to v1.0.0
Public URL of the repo agreed upon; README badges + LICENSE copyright reviewed

journal.note: All OSS scaffolding files created: CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, SECURITY.md, CHANGELOG.md (unreleased), .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/{bug_report,feature_request,spec_mismatch}.md, .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md. README Contributing section updated with CONTRIBUTING.md link. FUNDING.yml deferred to TTR-E09-S11.
